new_fix_this_run.description: Wired GraduatedRulesEngine.record_outcome(symbol, regime, side, won) as step 8 in FeedbackLoop.record_outcome(). This closes the feedback gap: 6 LLM graduated rules had times_applied>0 but times_correct=0 because the callback was never called. On restart, each trade close will now update rule accuracy. Rules can auto-retire at <35% accuracy (threshold: 10 applications). Tests: 56/56 feedback loop tests pass.
new_fix_this_run.impact: Activates accuracy tracking for 10 LLM graduated rules. Rules with 0% measured accuracy (hype_long_veto_v1, sol_long_veto_v1, hype_short_veto_v1, illiquid_regime_penalize_v1, btc_short_conf70_80_penalize_v1, conf_floor_70_v1) will now accumulate correct/incorrect counts. Auto-retirement will fire at 10+ applications below 35% accuracy.

high_priority_findings.finding: OUTCOME_CALLBACK_WIRED (P0 partial): bot/feedback/loop.py now calls GraduatedRulesEngine.record_outcome() as step 8 in FeedbackLoop.record_outcome(). This closes the accuracy feedback loop for 10 LLM graduated rules. 6 rules had times_applied>0 but times_correct=0 because the callback was missing. On restart, rule accuracy will accumulate correctly and auto-retirement will fire for consistently wrong rules (threshold: 10+ applications below 35%). DATA CORRUPTION NOTED: rule_1777922205_0 and night_session_block_v1 show times_correct > times_applied (impossible ratio) — likely from a bulk historical initialization artifact. The fix does not worsen this. 56/56 tests pass.
high_priority_findings.action: IMPLEMENTED. Verify on restart: '[FEEDBACK] Outcome recorded' log should be followed by graduated rules saving. Monitor hype_short_veto_v1 (3 applied, 0 correct) — if it stays at 0% after 10 more applications, it auto-retires.

high_priority_findings.finding: LLM_GRAD_RULE_DATA_CORRUPTION: rule_1777922205_0 has times_correct=21 with only times_applied=3 (700% impossible accuracy). night_session_block_v1 has times_correct=25 with times_applied=4 (625%). These were bulk-initialized from historical analysis, not from live outcome callbacks. The auto-retirement threshold (10 apps at <35%) will not be incorrectly triggered by these corrupted entries. However, the accuracy display for these rules is meaningless and should be reset.
high_priority_findings.action: Optional cleanup: reset times_correct=0 for corrupted rules (rule_1777922205_0, night_session_block_v1) and re-accumulate from live trading. Not urgent — corrupted values are stable and rules remain active.

high_priority_findings.finding: HYPE_SHORT_VETO_ACCURACY_RISK: hype_short_veto_v1 (HYPE SELL veto) has applied=3, correct=0 (0% accuracy). This means 3 times it vetoed HYPE SELL signals that would have LOST (veto was wrong — the trade would have been a loser, confirming the hypothesis). Wait — veto is correct when the vetoed trade LOSES. correct=0 means the 3 trades would have WON. This raises a critical question: are we wrongly blocking profitable HYPE SELL trades? SOL_LONG_VETO and HYPE_LONG_VETO both show similar (1 applied, 0 correct). After restart and 7+ more applications, these rules approach the 10-application auto-retire threshold.
high_priority_findings.action: On restart: monitor veto accuracy carefully. If HYPE SELL signals are being vetoed and the market moves in the vetoed direction (profitable), flag for human review. Consider shadow-mode for hype_short_veto_v1 after 10 applications.
